techniques.technique_name: Impair Defenses: Disable or Modify Cloud Logs
techniques.evidence_quote: the attacker authenticated to the AWS Console and navigated to the Services tab under the AWS Organizations service and began to disable the integration of six AWS services... DisableAWSServiceAccess... access-analyzer.amazonaws.com (IAM Access Analyzer)

techniques.technique_name: Use Alternate Authentication Material: Application Access Token
techniques.evidence_quote: Generating temporary STS credentials from long-lived access keys, which allows an attacker to authenticate to the AWS console even from long-lived credentials.

Identify the deletion of a high number of Lambda functions.

attack_chain.description: Attacker used a leaked long-term AWS IAM access key (AKIA*) belonging to a user in an AWS organization management account to gain initial access.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: attacker activity originating from a leaked long-term AWS access key (`AKIA*`). Within a 150-minute period, we detected five distinct IP addresses attempting to leverage this access key

attack_chain.description: Attacker enumerated AWS SES configuration via GetAccount, ListIdentities, and GetSendQuota API calls to assess the environment's email sending capabilities.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: SES enumeration through API calls such as GetAccount, ListIdentities, and GetSendQuota.

attack_chain.technique_name: Use Alternate Authentication Material: Application Access Token
attack_chain.description: Attacker used GetFederationToken to generate temporary STS credentials from the long-lived access key, enabling AWS console access.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: Generating temporary STS credentials from long-lived access keys, which allows an attacker to authenticate to the AWS console even from long-lived credentials.

attack_chain.description: Attacker created multiple IAM users (adminslabs, buckets488, s3s684, git-lab965, git-lab555) and granted them administrative permissions via AttachUserPolicy or AttachGroupPolicy, and created login profiles for console access.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: Creation of several IAM users, subsequently granted administrative permissions either directly through AttachUserPolicy or indirectly through AttachGroupPolicy. The attacker sometimes attempted to create a login profile on the IAM user to facilitate using the AWS console.

attack_chain.description: Attacker attempted to create EC2 security group named 'Administratorsz' (JavaGhost TTP) and created IAM login profiles to grant console access to newly created users.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: Attempt to create an EC2 security group called `Administratorsz` with the description `We Are There But Not Visible`, which has been attributed to the JavaGhost group.

attack_chain.description: Attacker deployed a Lambda function named 'buckets555' with malicious code capable of dynamically creating IAM users on demand, then wired it to an HTTP API Gateway to create a persistent 'backdoor-as-a-service' mechanism survivable after credential revocation.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: the attacker created a Lambda function named `buckets555`... this Lambda function ran code with the capability to create IAM users dynamically, on demand. This effectively creates a 'persistence-as-a-service' mechanism: The attacker, even after the compromised credentials are revoked, is able to perform external HTTP requests to the API Gateway and dynamically create further malicious IAM users.

attack_chain.description: Attacker enumerated the AWS Identity Center (SSO) instance, inspecting SSO configurations, users, groups, and applications.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: We observed the attacker enumerating the SSO instance to look at SSO configurations, users, groups, and applications.

attack_chain.technique_name: Impair Defenses: Disable or Modify Cloud Logs
attack_chain.description: Attacker used DisableAWSServiceAccess to disable trusted access for six AWS organization-level services, including IAM Access Analyzer, AWS Systems Manager, and CloudFormation StackSets, likely to weaken security controls for future operations.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: the attacker authenticated to the AWS Console and navigated to the Services tab under the AWS Organizations service and began to disable the integration of six AWS services... DisableAWSServiceAccess... access-analyzer.amazonaws.com (IAM Access Analyzer)

attack_chain.description: Attacker created a new IAM Identity Center group named 'secure' and user named 'Secret', added the user to the group, and assigned a new permission set to the group to establish SSO-based persistence.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: they created a group called `secure` and a user called `Secret`, which the attacker added to their group, and assigned a new permission set to that group.

attack_chain.description: Attacker modified the SSO MFA configuration to permit sign-in without MFA, and extended the Amazon Q Developer session duration to 90 days to facilitate long-term access.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: they modified the MFA configuration of the SSO instance to allow themselves to sign in without MFA. They then extended the session duration for Amazon Q Developer to 90 days.

attack_chain.description: Attacker successfully signed in to the AWS console using the newly created SSO user 'Secret' via a password-only flow (no MFA), confirming successful SSO persistence.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: we observed a successful sign-in event associated with a password-only sign-in flow for the newly created user `Secret`.

initial_access_vector: Leaked long-term AWS IAM access key (AKIA* prefix) associated with an IAM user in an AWS organization management account
persistence_mechanism: Multiple persistence mechanisms: (1) New admin IAM users with login profiles; (2) Lambda function + API Gateway 'backdoor-as-a-service' enabling dynamic IAM user creation post-credential revocation; (3) New AWS Identity Center user 'Secret' with SSO permission set and MFA disabled; (4) Amazon Q Developer session extended to 90 days
privilege_escalation: Attacker leveraged the management account's highly privileged leaked key to directly attach AdministratorAccess managed policy to newly created IAM users and groups, and assigned permission sets via AWS Identity Center
